present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly wakes up at six and opens the curtains wide.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly wakes up at six and immediately falls back asleep.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly woke up early and sang to herself in the kitchen.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly woke up and put his slippers on the wrong feet.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will wake up early and water her roses.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will wake up late. He always does.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has woken up at six every day for forty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has woken up on the wrong side of the bed. Again.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly stretches her arms wide and greets the morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly stretches and knocks the lamp clean off the bedside table.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly stretched for ten minutes and felt completely wonderful.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly stretched too far and rolled right off the bed.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will stretch every morning to keep herself healthy.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will stretch and break something. Probably the lamp again.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has stretched every morning since her yoga class began.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has never stretched properly in his entire life.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly brushes her teeth for exactly two minutes every morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly brushes his hair with the toothbrush by mistake.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly brushed her hair and hummed a quiet little tune.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly brushed his teeth with the children's strawberry toothpaste.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will brush her hair before her morning walk.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will brush his teeth and forget to rinse. As usual.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has brushed her teeth twice already — just to be certain.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has brushed the wrong thing in the wrong room again.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly makes a pot of tea for the whole family every morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly makes coffee in the teapot and wonders why it tastes strange.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly made perfectly buttered toast for absolutely everyone.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly made tea without the teabag. He drank it anyway.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will make a lovely breakfast for the grandchildren.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will make something in the kitchen that nobody can identify.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has made tea every morning for thirty happy years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has made every breakfast slightly wrong since 1989.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly eats a boiled egg and reads the paper cheerfully.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly eats the dog's biscuits thinking they are cereal.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly ate a big breakfast and felt completely ready for anything.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly ate the whole loaf before anyone else came downstairs.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will eat her porridge with honey and fresh berries.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will eat breakfast standing over the sink and call it efficient.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has eaten a proper breakfast every single morning of her life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has eaten cereal without milk and called it a lifestyle choice.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly reads the newspaper with her morning cup of tea.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly reads yesterday's paper and thinks all the news is new.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly read every article and completed the crossword perfectly.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly read the TV guide and missed his favourite programme.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will read the paper before her morning walk.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will read his horoscope and take it very seriously.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has read every copy of the local paper this year.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has read the same article four times and still cannot recall it.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly feeds the birds in the garden every single morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly feeds the birds and accidentally gives them his own breakfast.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly fed the robins and they sang just for her.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly fed the squirrel and it followed him all the way back inside.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will feed the birds before the grandchildren arrive.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will feed something this morning. He is not entirely sure what.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has fed the garden birds every morning all winter long.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has fed the neighbour's cat by accident for six weeks.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly walks two miles before nine every single morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly walks to the end of the road and forgets where he was going.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly walked past the bakery and waved to the whole queue.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly walked into the wrong house and sat down for tea.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will walk along the river and count the ducks.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will walk in the wrong direction and discover a new village.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has walked this path every morning for twenty years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has walked past the postbox seventeen times this week.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly meets her neighbour Pat and stops for a lovely chat.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly meets people and immediately forgets their names.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly met the new family on the corner and invited them to tea.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly met his neighbour and called him the wrong name for ten minutes.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will meet her friend Dorothy at the garden gate.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will meet someone important and have spinach in his teeth.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has met every single person in this village by name.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has met Mrs Bubbly's sister twelve times and still asks who she is.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly buys fresh bread from the baker every morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly buys three things they do not need and forgets the one they do.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly bought the last Victoria sponge and shared it with everyone.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly bought decaffeinated coffee and said nothing for an entire month.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will buy flowers for the kitchen and cake for Thursday.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will buy the wrong brand and be very proud of the saving.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has bought something lovely for every neighbour this month.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has bought four copies of the same book. He owns them all.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly calls her daughter every single morning without fail.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly calls the wrong number and has a twenty-minute conversation anyway.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly called to check on her elderly friend down the lane.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly called the dentist instead of the garage. He kept talking.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will call her sister before the grandchildren arrive.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will call at exactly the wrong moment.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has called every family member on their birthday.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has called himself instead of his son. Twice this week.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly tidies one shelf and considers the whole house done.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly tidied the kitchen and it looked like an actual show home.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly tidied his newspapers into a pile and then sat directly on them.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will tidy the spare room before the grandchildren arrive.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will tidy his things into a bag and hide the bag.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has tidied this house every single morning for decades.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has tidied nothing and moved everything slightly to the left.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly washes the clothes and hangs them out perfectly.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly washes everything on the wrong temperature. Every single time.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly washed and ironed the curtains in one busy afternoon.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly washed his red socks together with the white shirts.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will wash the windows until they shine like mirrors.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will wash something he definitely should not and completely shrink it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has washed everything in the house at least twice this week.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has washed his phone. It was in his shirt pocket.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly helps by digging in completely the wrong spot.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly helped little Rosie tie her very first pair of shoes.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly helped Oliver with his homework and they both got it wrong.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will help the children bake biscuits this afternoon.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will help with the baking and eat far more than he bakes.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has helped this family through every single difficulty.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has helped in ways that always need a second attempt.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly plays in the garden with her grandchildren all morning.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly plays on the children's tablet and cannot figure out how to stop.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly played hide and seek and was absolutely brilliant at hiding.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly played chase and tripped over the garden hose.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will play every single game the grandchildren suggest.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will play the board game and quietly change the rules.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has played with these grandchildren every weekend for years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has played every game wrong and enjoyed himself greatly.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly cooked a full roast for eleven people and loved every minute.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly cooked pasta without any water and called it a dry dish.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will cook a big lunch for the whole family on Sunday.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will cook something entirely unrecognisable and be very proud.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has cooked over fifteen thousand meals in this kitchen.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has cooked one truly successful meal. He mentions it often.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly talks with her daughter about everything and nothing.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly talks through the whole film and ruins the ending completely.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly talked for two hours and everyone wanted to stay even longer.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly talked about the weather for forty-five uninterrupted minutes.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will talk to her neighbour over the fence this afternoon.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will talk and say nothing of use. Happily.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has talked her daughter through every single difficult decision.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has talked about his knee for so long it now has its own story arc.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly checks her messages and replies to every single one.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly checks his phone and accidentally likes a post from 2013.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly checked the family WhatsApp and sent a dozen heart emojis.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly checked his messages and replied to the entirely wrong thread.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will check on her friends online and send encouragement.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will check his phone and accidentally video-call the whole family.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has checked the family group chat with enormous enthusiasm.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has checked his phone upside down and still replied confidently.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly posts a beautiful photo of her roses every afternoon.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly posts a photo of his lunch every day. Nobody asks him to.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly posted a recipe and received five hundred lovely comments.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly posted a photo of his feet and thought it was the garden.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will post a photo of the grandchildren at the park.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will post something confusing with the wrong filter applied.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has posted something uplifting every single week.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has posted sixteen blurry photos of the ceiling.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sits in her favourite chair and reads her book.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly sits in someone else's chair and refuses to move.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sat in the garden until the sun moved behind the tree.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly sat in the wet garden chair and did not notice for a full hour.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will sit by the window and knit all afternoon.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will sit down to rest and sleep until teatime.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has sat in this very spot every afternoon since the roses bloomed.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has sat on the cat, the remote, and the post all in one week.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly rests for twenty minutes and wakes up at five o'clock.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly rested briefly and then cheerfully started the ironing.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will rest after the grandchildren go home.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will rest and somehow make it louder than the television.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has rested properly and feels completely refreshed.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has rested so much today that nobody is sure he is actually awake.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly waters the roses and also the neighbour's fresh washing.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly watered everything while singing quietly to herself.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will water her tomatoes and check on the sweet peas.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly picks the wrong flowers and brings home the neighbour's prize dahlias.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will find the scissors because she always knows where they are.

perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has listened to the same CD on repeat since 1997.

perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has brought chaos to three different dinner parties this year.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has poured tea for more people than she could ever count.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has told the best stories at every dinner table she has ever sat at.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly laughs loudly and freely and fills every room with joy.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly laughs at his own jokes before he has even finished telling them.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has laughed every single day of her long and remarkable life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has laughed in all the wrong silences and all the right ones.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has cleared up after every family meal without a single complaint.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has cleared things into the wrong cupboards three times this evening.

present.mr: Mr Bubbly says goodbye and then keeps talking for another thirty minutes.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has said the most perfect things at every single departure.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has said goodbye for so long the guests have had to leave without him finishing.

perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has filled his slippers with water by leaving them in the bathroom.

perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has put his keys in the fridge, his phone in the cupboard, and his slippers in the hall.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly hears the owls outside and feels deeply content.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly hears something suspicious and gets up to investigate the cat.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly heard the rain on the window and pulled the blanket closer.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly heard a noise, went downstairs, and woke the dog, the cat, and the neighbours.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will hear the birds in the morning and smile.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will hear something at three o'clock and be awake until five.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has heard every sound this old house makes and loves every one.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has heard things in this house that nobody else can explain.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly falls asleep within minutes, calm and perfectly content.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly falls asleep mid-sentence and begins snoring immediately.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly fell asleep thinking of her grandchildren's laughter.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly fell asleep in his clothes and did not notice until morning.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will fall asleep knowing she has had a truly wonderful day.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will fall asleep the moment his head touches the pillow and deny it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has fallen asleep peacefully every night this week.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has fallen asleep in his chair, on the sofa, at the table, and once in the bath.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly dreams of gardens, dancing, and her grandchildren's faces.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly dreams of finding things he has lost and immediately losing them again.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly dreamed she was young again, dancing by the sea.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly dreamed he was a famous chef and woke up very hungry.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will dream beautifully tonight. She always does.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will dream about something confusing and wake up even more tired.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has dreamed the most beautiful dreams her whole life.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has dreamed the most peculiar dreams and woken the whole household.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly sleeps deeply and peacefully through the whole night.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly sleeps loudly and peacefully and does not know the difference.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly slept eight full hours and woke feeling completely renewed.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly slept through the fire alarm, the thunderstorm, and the dog.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will sleep well because she has had a full and happy day.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will sleep like a man with a completely clear conscience.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has slept well every night because she fills her days with love.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has slept through more of his own life than he suspects.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shares everything she has and everything she is.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly shares stories that grow longer with every telling.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shared her last biscuit with a complete stranger at the bus stop.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly shared someone's umbrella without asking. They were standing under it.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will share her garden, her table, and her heart.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will share information nobody asked for with great enthusiasm.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has shared decades of joy, warmth, and extraordinary cake.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has shared the wrong Wi-Fi password confidently every day for three years.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly keeps every letter, every drawing, every little thing.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly keeps losing things he had firmly in his hand thirty seconds ago.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly kept every birthday card the grandchildren ever made her.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly kept the wrong receipts and threw all the important ones away.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will keep smiling through every season of her long life.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will keep meaning to fix that shelf. For another three years.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has kept this family together through every storm.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has kept every newspaper since 1987. They are all in the garage.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gives without thinking and always has more to give.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly gives advice nobody asked for with absolute confidence.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly gave her warmest coat to the neighbour who was cold.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly gave a very long speech at Christmas and missed dinner completely.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will give every grandchild the most thoughtful gift imaginable.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will give up halfway through wrapping and use a bag instead.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has given everything to this family and loved every moment.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has given away the good umbrella. He cannot recall to whom.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly remembers every birthday, anniversary, and first day of school.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly remembers his National Service perfectly but not what he had for breakfast.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly remembered to call the plumber before it became truly urgent.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly remembered it was bin day on Thursday. It was Wednesday.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will remember this day as one of the very best ones.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will remember tomorrow. Or the day after. He will look into it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has remembered every kindness anyone has ever shown her.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has remembered things that never happened and forgotten things that did.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly carries kindness with her wherever she goes.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly carries three bags everywhere and manages to lose all of them.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly carried fresh flowers to the hospital every single week.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly carried the shopping and forgot to bring the shopping bag.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will carry on inspiring everyone who knows her.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will carry the wrong box to the wrong place with great conviction.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has carried this family on her shoulders with complete joy.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has carried an umbrella for twenty years. It has never once been used.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly opens the curtains and the world looks beautiful again.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly opens the wrong drawer looking for his glasses every morning.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly opened the kitchen window and breathed in the morning air.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly opened the fridge, closed it, and opened it again immediately.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will open her arms wide to another wonderful day.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will open his eyes and wonder what month it is.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has opened every single morning with gratitude.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has opened the biscuit tin at six in the morning and called it breakfast.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly knows everyone's name in the whole village.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly knows very little and quietly forgets the rest.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly knew exactly what to say to cheer absolutely anyone up.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly knew the way home and turned left anyway.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will know the answer before the question has even ended.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will not know what day it is. As usual.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has known her best friend for over fifty beautiful years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has known about this problem for six months and done nothing.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly shows everyone how kindness can change everything.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly shows up at the wrong time in the wrong place.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly showed her paintings at the village library exhibition.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly showed up to the wedding on completely the wrong day.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will show her roses at the summer flower show.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will show everyone precisely how not to do it.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has shown so much kindness over all these years.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has shown that chaos is possible anywhere, at any time.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly loves this life, this family, and every ordinary day.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly loves Mrs Bubbly more than he will ever properly say.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly loved every single chapter of this long and beautiful story.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly loved Mrs Bubbly from the very first day. He still does.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will love this family with every breath she has left to give.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will love Mrs Bubbly until he forgets everything else. Which is soon.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has loved this ordinary, extraordinary life completely.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has loved Mrs Bubbly for fifty years and would do it all again.

present.mrs: Mrs Bubbly lives every day as if it is a gift. Because it is.
present.mr: Mr Bubbly lives in a state of gentle confusion and complete happiness.

past.mrs: Mrs Bubbly lived a life so full that not a single moment was wasted.
past.mr: Mr Bubbly lived alongside Mrs Bubbly and somehow that was enough for everything.

future.mrs: Mrs Bubbly will live every remaining day with joy and with purpose.
future.mr: Mr Bubbly will live the rest of his days happily — and misplace his keys every single one of them.

perfect.mrs: Mrs Bubbly has lived a life overflowing with love, laughter, and great cake.
perfect.mr: Mr Bubbly has lived a wonderful life. He just cannot always remember all of it.
